evaluator.note: Benchmark v2. Blind subagent: packet-only context (prompt.md, diff-drift-report.md, raw-git-diff.patch); dispatched by the maintainer's agent session on 2026-06-10. Answer recorded verbatim.

findings.title: Signature verification replaced with non-verifying decode
findings.evidence: Raw diff: `- return verify(token, PUBLIC_KEY);` replaced by `+ return decode(token);` using `jwt-tiny-decode`. Decoding does not check the signature, so forged tokens authenticate successfully.

findings.title: Token format validation neutralized (regex widened and guard disabled)
findings.evidence: Raw diff: `/^[A-Za-z0-9_\-]{32,}$/` became `/.*/`, and the rejection branch became `if (false) { throw new Error("Malformed token"); }` — dead code, so no input is ever rejected. Diff Drift rated the disabled guard low, but combined with the regex change it fully removes input rejection in an auth path.

findings.title: Undeclared dependency jwt-tiny-decode introduced into auth path
findings.evidence: Raw diff adds `import { decode } from "jwt-tiny-decode";` with no corresponding package.json change in the diff — possible hallucinated or supply-chain-risky package, and it is the new sole basis of token acceptance.

findings.title: Input sanitization call removed
findings.evidence: Raw diff removes `sanitizeInput(token);` from validateToken, while the same change makes all token strings pass validation — unsanitized attacker-controlled input now flows onward.

findings.title: Logger redaction emptied and level lowered to debug
findings.evidence: Raw diff: `level: "info", redact: ["req.headers.authorization", "token"]` became `level: "debug", redact: []` — authorization headers and tokens can now reach log sinks, unrelated to the stated token-validation refactor.

notes: routes/session.ts changes are whitespace/blank-line only; Diff Drift's 'Formatting only · 0 active flags' classification matches the raw diff.
All six Diff Drift flags are corroborated by the raw git diff; no false positives observed in this packet.
No files were skipped as too large in this report.
The combination of changes (accept any string, decode without verify, disable logging redaction) is a classic auth-bypass pattern and goes well beyond a 'refactor' as the branch name suggests.
